user_query:
I need a quick local CRM snapshot from our data files. Please read the inputs at input/contacts.csv, input/deals.csv, and input/config.json and produce three outputs under output/:

1) output/pipeline_report.json — Summarize deal counts and total value per stage across the standard stages [prospect, qualified, proposal, negotiation, closed-won, closed-lost]. Include all six stages in the output with their counts and total_value, even if zero. Also include grand_total_count and grand_total_value across all stages. Values should be numeric (not strings).

2) output/followups_due_7d.md — List only follow-ups for OPEN stages (prospect, qualified, proposal, negotiation) that are due within the next 7 days relative to the anchor date in input/config.json (inclusive of the anchor day and the 7th day). Sort by due date ascending. Use this exact format:
- First line: "Follow-ups due in next 7 days (anchor: YYYY-MM-DD)" where YYYY-MM-DD is the anchor date from config.json
- Second line (columns header): "due_date | deal_title | contact_name | note"
- Subsequent lines: one per follow-up in the format "YYYY-MM-DD | Deal Title | Contact Name | Note"

3) output/deal_tags.csv — A CSV with header "title,tags" and one row per deal, sorted by title ascending. For each row, output the deal title and a semicolon-separated list of that deal's tags, normalized to lower-case, trimmed, de-duplicated, and sorted alphabetically. Example row: "Acme Pilot,pilot;priority;saas".

Assumptions and rules:
- Consolidate contacts by email to resolve contact_name for deals. If a deal’s contact_email has a match in contacts.csv, use that contact’s name.
- Parse values as numbers for totals.
- Consider only the specified open stages for the follow-ups list. Pipeline report should include all stages.
- Use input/config.json only for the anchor date (treat it as "today").
- Write the three files exactly to the paths above under output/.

Let me know if anything in the inputs is unclear.
